task_yaml.task:
I'm building an onboarding guide for new engineers joining our Flask
work and I want to highlight the 10 hottest files — the ones that
change the most and break the most. The tarball is at
/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/flask_repo.tar.gz and I want the
answer based purely on that snapshot. Use the workspace's
code-analysis skill for the static analysis, the git skill to
walk the commit history, and the log-analysis skill to parse and
aggregate the log output.

Look at the most recent 2 years of commit history in the repo. Score
each file with
  hotspot = churn * log(1 + bugfix)
where churn is insertions plus deletions over that window, and bugfix
is the number of commits whose message contains "fix" or "bug",
case-insensitive. Sort descending by hotspot, and break ties on the
rounded hotspot value by file path alphabetically.

Save the result to /tmp_workspace/results/hotspots.md. Lead with a
10-row markdown table with columns Rank, File, Churn, BugFix, Hotspot.
After the table, give me a short read on the surrounding picture so I
can answer the follow-up questions onboarding folks always ask.
Specifically I want a quick note on which contributors are concentrated
where — is one or two committers carrying everything, or is it
distributed — where the churn really lives, long-tail across many
files versus a spike on a few; what subsystems get the most bug fixes,
for example extensions, blueprints, templating, routing; and whether
the picture has shifted between the early half and the recent half of
the 2-year window. A few short paragraphs or a small table per topic
is fine, just keep it tight.
